translation_findings.issue: zh choices C and D start with raw katakana markers 'гӮҰпјҺ' and 'гӮЁпјҺ' instead of normalized 'CпјҺ/DпјҺ'. Choices A and B are correctly normalized to 'AпјҺ/BпјҺ'.

All four zh choices retain raw katakana markers 'гӮўпјҺ/гӮӨпјҺ/гӮҰпјҺ/гӮЁпјҺ' instead of A/B/C/D uppercase. en column is also fully un-normalized for index in stem; choices in en use A./B./C./D. correctly but zh fully leaks.
All four zh choices use Chinese ordinals з”І/д№ҷ/дёҷ/дёҒ ('з”ІпјҺ/д№ҷпјҺ/дёҷпјҺ/дёҒпјҺ'). Spec mandates A/B/C/D uppercase across zh and en. en is correct; zh is wholly non-compliant.
All four zh choices use з”І/д№ҷ/дёҷ/дёҒ instead of A/B/C/D.
Choice D en 'Steal data.' drops the infinitive 'To' present in other three choices ('To seize.../To disrupt.../To tamper...'). Minor parallelism inconsistency.
